source_text: and so you might want to have the replicas of those songs, uh and and maybe even the metadata, be more locally, uh, represented more more locally so that um, you can get to it faster, you don't have to cross an ocean to get to the to the data, um and uh, you know to reach it. So kind of a geo-aware strategy of data placement and possibly replication strategy that might be a, um, a refinement I think that I would might make to this design to make it just a little bit more um, uh, performant effective uh etc etc. So, yeah, does that does that make sense? Yeah, yeah, I think that thing is just interesting point to add. It's a good idea to wrap up your answer by referring back to the requirements laid out at the beginning of the interview, and confirming that your design meets them. If you have time, it's always nice to think big and take a quick look at the problem from a different dimension that could make it more complex. Mark did this with his geo-location idea. He didn't go into detail, but another interviewer might have explored this further
reference_translation: 所以你可能希望这些歌的副本，甚至是元数据，更多地存储在本地， 以便更快访问。这样就不用大费周折去获取数据。这算是一种考虑地理因素的数据存放策略和复制策略， 在我看来这可能是一种改进，让这个设计更加高效能一点，等等。 这样有意义吗？有的，我觉得添加的这个点很有意思。通过对照面试开头提出的要求来总结回答是个不错的做法，也可以确保你的设计符合要求。 如果时间允许，可以大胆设想，并从另一个维度快速审视问题，这样可以让它更复合。马克提出了关于地理位置的想法。他没有具体阐述，但另一位可能深入探索了，
